mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, directly boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based demand expansion, which compresses input cost margins and sustains revenue growth, positively impacting S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 50 units and INDPRO rises > 1.0 index points within 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the individual series show identical predictive power without statistical improvement from the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production eases supply bottlenecks and moderates inflation expectations, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy and supporting short-term equity multiples.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in over 65% of observations during identified recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in retail sales exceeding 2.0 percent signals a demand contraction that forces analysts to downgrade forward earnings estimates, directly depressing S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the retail sales decline, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over lagged GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently exceeding 2.5 percent annualized sustains corporate revenue expansion and profit margin stability, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-GDP quarters do not significantly outperform low-GDP quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms structural labor demand, which reduces recession fears and supports equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy by at least 5% compared to a baseline model using PAYEMS alone, or the joint signal predicts negative returns in over 50% of confirmed labor tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5 percent, real interest rates turn negative, triggering inflation-hedge rotation out of equities into commodities and depressing S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS move in this configuration, or the signal shows no statistical significance after controlling for VIX levels.
